mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate signals monetary easing that reduces discount rates applied to equity valuations. A 3-month rolling decline in FEDFUNDS produces positive S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days as market reprices growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.61% as of March 2008 reflects Fed easing cycle initiated in September 2007
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle following credit shock
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in < 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS declines over preceding 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity. When UNRATE increases by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months, S&P 500 forward returns turn negative within 5 trading days as earnings expectations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.1% in March 2008, elevated from 4.7% in December 2007
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in < 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 12-month rolling window following trigger activation.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales decline signals weakening consumer demand and reduced corporate revenue growth. When RSAFS declines month-over-month, S&P 500 forward returns are negative over 20 trading days as consensus earnings estimates are revised downward.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 381,416M in March 2008, reflecting consumer spending stress from housing crisis
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in > 45% of instances following month-over-month RSAFS decline, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production decline combined with rising unemployment indicates supply-side weakness and demand destruction. When INDPRO falls while UNRATE rises simultaneously, S&P 500 forward returns are negative over 60 trading days as recession probability increases.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month decline AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 2 months
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 111.91 in February 2008, UNRATE rising from 4.9% to 5.1% in 2 months
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in < 60% of jointly triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 24-month regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts collapse signals severe demand destruction in construction and related sectors. When HOUST falls below 1,000 thousand units (annualized), S&P 500 forward returns are negative over 20 trading days as financial sector stress and multiplier effects propagate.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,065 thousand units in February 2008, near critical threshold
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in > 50% of instances when HOUST < 1000, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 12-month rolling window in housing-stressed regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment decline signals labor market deterioration and reduced household income. When PAYEMS declines month-over-month, S&P 500 forward returns are negative over 20 trading days as consumer spending and corporate earnings face headwinds.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 137,846 thousand in March 2008, declining from peak of 138,365 in December 2007
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in > 55% of instances following PAYEMS month-over-month decline, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL combined with monetary tightening (high FEDFUNDS) creates stagflationary pressure. When CPIAUCSL rises while FEDFUNDS remains elevated above 2.5%, S&P 500 forward returns are negative over 60 trading days as real returns compress and growth expectations deteriorate.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 212.571 in February 2008, FEDFUNDS at 2.61% in March 2008
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in > 55% of jointly triggered instances, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 24-month regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured by GDPC1 combined with declining retail sales indicates demand-side weakness despite recent growth. When GDPC1 growth slows to < 1.5% annualized while RSAFS declines, S&P 500 forward returns are negative over 20 trading days as recession risk rises.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 1.5% AND RSAFS month-over-month decline
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11,675.7B as of Q4 2007, RSAFS declining in early 2008
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in > 50% of jointly triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 12-month regressions.
